title: NATO Faces Crisis Over US Commitment
source: The Guardian - The Economist (Web Edition)  23 May 2026
content:
The fatal flaw in a military commander, as suggested by a 19th-century Prussian general, is obstinacy and resistance against better judgment. For a modern example of this behavior, political analysts point to Mark Rutte, the current secretary-general of NATO. For months, Rutte has refused to publicly admit that the transatlantic alliance is dangerously close to falling apart. The organization has served as the cornerstone of European security for more than seven decades. However, maintaining his optimistic facade has required a level of willful blindness that ignores shifting geopolitical realities.

Rutte continues to ignore repeated statements from the United States casting severe doubt on its future commitment to European defense. American threats to seize Greenland and unexpected cuts to troop deployments have spurred several European countries to secretly plan for a strategic divorce. The United States is also postponing crucial weapons deliveries to restock its own depleted arsenals elsewhere. Consequently, Europe must urgently take responsibility for its own conventional defense and consider forming new regional alliances. Renovating the American-led security structure will be difficult and costly, but remaining unprepared is a far greater risk.
